Japan.name: Japan
Japan.rivals: South Korea remains Japan's fiercest football rival, with the rivalry stretching back decades and carrying significant historical and cultural weight. Encounters with Australia have also become major fixtures in modern Asian football.

timeline.event: Win their first AFC Asian Cup.
Qualify for their first World Cup.
Reach the Round of 16 as co-hosts of the World Cup.
Reach the Round of 16 again in South Africa.
Defeat both Germany and Spain to top their group before narrowly losing to Croatia on penalties.
Qualify comfortably and arrive as Asia's highest-ranked representative.

players.name: Kaoru Mitoma
players.club: Brighton winger whose dribbling makes him one of Asia's most dangerous attackers.

players.name: Takefusa Kubo
players.club: creative playmaker and the face of Japan's next generation.

players.name: Wataru Endo
players.club: captain and midfield leader whose tactical discipline underpins the entire team.

Jordan.name: Jordan
Jordan.rivals: Iraq is Jordan's most emotionally charged regional rivalry, while encounters with Saudi Arabia and Syria have traditionally drawn significant interest across the Arab football landscape.

timeline.event: Jordan plays its first official international match.
Reaches the Asian Cup quarter-finals for the first time.
Falls one step short of World Cup qualification, losing an intercontinental play-off to Uruguay.
Reaches the AFC Asian Cup final, the greatest achievement in the nation's football history.
Secures qualification for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Makes its World Cup debut.

players.name: Mousa Al-Tamari
players.club: the face of Jordanian football and the country's first major European success story.

players.name: Yazan Al-Naimat
players.club: prolific forward and hero of the Asian Cup run.

players.name: Mahmoud Al-Mardi
players.club: experienced attacker who consistently delivers in major tournaments.

Korea Republic.name: Korea Republic
Korea Republic.rivals: Japan is the defining rivalry in Asian football, shaped by geography, history and sporting competition. Encounters with Iran have also become some of the continent's most important fixtures over the last three decades.

timeline.event: Win the inaugural AFC Asian Cup.
Begin a streak of consecutive World Cup qualifications that continues today.
Reach the World Cup semi-finals on home soil, the best-ever performance by an Asian nation.
Win Olympic bronze in London.
Reach the Round of 16 after defeating Portugal.
Qualify for an eleventh consecutive World Cup.

players.name: Son Heung-min
players.club: Tottenham Hotspur captain and the greatest Korean footballer of the modern era.

players.name: Lee Kang-in
players.club: creative playmaker capable of deciding matches with a single moment.

players.name: Kim Min-jae
players.club: defensive leader and one of Asia's most accomplished defenders.

Mexico.name: Mexico
Mexico.rivals: The United States is Mexico's defining modern rivalry, reflecting the shifting balance of power within CONCACAF. Matches against Argentina have also developed special significance due to repeated World Cup eliminations.

timeline.event: Participate in the inaugural FIFA World Cup.
Reach the quarter-finals while hosting the World Cup.
Reach the quarter-finals again as hosts.
Win their first CONCACAF Gold Cup.
Fail to progress from the group stage for the first time since 1978.
Become the first nation to host matches at three separate men's World Cups.

players.name: Santiago Giménez
players.club: the striker expected to lead Mexico's next generation.

players.name: Edson Álvarez
players.club: midfield anchor and emotional leader.

players.name: Julián Quiñones
players.club: dynamic attacker whose emergence added another dimension to the side.

Morocco.name: Morocco
Morocco.rivals: Algeria is Morocco's fiercest football rivalry, extending beyond sport and reflecting decades of regional competition. Egypt and Tunisia are also major continental rivals.

timeline.event: Become the first African nation to earn a World Cup point.
Become the first African nation to win a World Cup group.
Reach the Africa Cup of Nations final.
Become the first African and Arab semi-finalists in World Cup history.
Qualify comfortably for the 2026 World Cup.
Arrive with realistic ambitions of another deep tournament run.

players.name: Achraf Hakimi
players.club: captain, leader and one of the world's best attacking full-backs.

players.name: Sofyan Amrabat
players.club: the midfield heartbeat of the Atlas Lions.

players.name: Brahim Díaz
players.club: creative attacker capable of unlocking any defence.

Netherlands.name: Netherlands
Netherlands.rivals: Germany is the Netherlands' defining football rivalry, rooted in both history and some of the sport's most iconic matches. Belgium, in the Low Countries derby, remains another major opponent.

timeline.event: Reach their first World Cup final under Johan Cruyff and Total Football.
Reach a second consecutive World Cup final.
Win the European Championship, their only major international title.
Reach a third World Cup final before losing to Spain.
Finish third after defeating Brazil 3-0.
Enter the tournament with another highly talented generation built around a strong defensive core.

players.name: Virgil van Dijk
players.club: captain and defensive leader.

players.name: Xavi Simons
players.club: the creative focal point of the next Dutch generation.

players.name: Jeremie Frimpong
players.club: one of the most dangerous attacking wing-backs in world football.

New Zealand.name: New Zealand
New Zealand.rivals: Australia remains New Zealand's biggest sporting rival across all codes, while recent competitive meetings with New Caledonia have gained significance within Oceania.

timeline.event: Makes its first World Cup appearance in Spain.
Goes unbeaten at the World Cup, drawing all three group matches including one against reigning champions Italy.
Wins the OFC Nations Cup.
Qualifies for the intercontinental play-off but falls short against Costa Rica.
Secures direct qualification as Oceania receives an automatic World Cup berth for the first time.
Appears at its third FIFA World Cup.

players.name: Chris Wood
players.club: Nottingham Forest captain and New Zealand's all-time leading goalscorer.

players.name: Liberato Cacace
players.club: attacking full-back and key creative outlet.

players.name: Tyler Bindon
players.club: highly rated young defender viewed as a cornerstone of the future.

Norway.name: Norway
Norway.rivals: Sweden is Norway's traditional football rival, while Denmark forms part of the fiercely contested Scandinavian triangle.

timeline.event: Makes its World Cup debut.
Returns to the World Cup after a 56-year absence.
Reaches the Round of 16 after defeating Brazil.
Appears at the European Championship, then begins a long absence from major tournaments.
Qualifies for the 2026 World Cup behind a golden generation.
Returns to football's biggest stage after 28 years away.

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.club: Manchester City striker and one of the world's most prolific goalscorers.

players.name: Martin Ødegaard
players.club: Arsenal captain and creative heartbeat of the team.

players.name: Antonio Nusa
players.club: explosive young winger regarded as Norway's next breakout star.
